reviews.review_msg: Andrew is one of the best profs I've had at SFU! I took bus 303 with him and an year later took 478 with him. On the first day of the  478 class he recognized me and remembered my name! Loved the class discussions. He really knows his stuff and constantly challenges you to support your arguments. Take a class with him. Highly recommend him.

He's smart, but has trouble concluding things and driving home important points. It seems like he asks a lot of questions and doesn't give a lot of answers. I'd like to see him offer more of his own insight. Otherwise high quality instructor and challenges you to think about your answers. He's picky, so don't overlook simple things (slide design)

Agree with everything 3/29/10 said. Very interactive class, participation marks mean you have to contribute at least once in a while. Expects you to read assigned case studies + text so it's a lot of reading. Pop quizzes were like roulette bc it rarely covered even half the readings. Take-home exams sound nice but he marks hard bc you get a week.

Andrew is an exceptional professor. I have never taken a more interactive 300-level class. Class discussions consist of real-life issues and raise your awareness level to the things around you greatly. He does his best to help students and is a truly caring guy. Take home MT and Final. Take this class with him!

Assigns book chapters + extra case studies for reading so dont take if you hate reading. Class is 1/2 open discussion, 1/2 lecture. Pop quizzes. Will mark hard & expect lots for take home exams cus you had lots of time to work on it. Overall a nice guy interested in his work but wasn't very good at giving direction for exams or group report.
The best professor I've ever had!  What he teaches is practical, interesting, yet challenging to apply.  Made me very interested in pursuing Strategy as a career choice.

Andrew is an awesome prof.  Extremely knowledgeable and also very interested in the subject itself. I like the way that he asks the student to provide constant feedback for his lecture so that he'll make certain adjustment to his method of teaching. By the way, he graduated from Stanford and MIT!!!

Outstanding - engaging prof, hands down best in SFU Business for strategy.  Not a fan of him selecting groups though...  beware if your a born and raised Canadian - youll be editing endlessly as a result - take the class nonetheless
Awesome Prof - Totally take this course with him if you get the chance!
